description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its length. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, likely intended to provide traction or a decorative effect. The block is solid and has a dark, stone-like appearance with a slightly mottled texture, suggesting it is made from granite or a similar durable material. The sides are smooth and unadorned, contrasting with the textured top. The overall form is robust and utilitarian, suitable for use as a barrier or obstacle in urban environments. Its substantial mass and hard surface indicate it is designed to withstand significant force and outdoor exposure.
description: A solid rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface and smooth, dark sides. The grooved pattern provides both traction and a distinctive visual texture.
description_view_0: Front view showing the grooved top surface with the stripes running horizontally and the smooth, solid front face.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the stripes running vertically from this perspective.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the grooved top with horizontal stripes and a plain rear face.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the vertical grooves and the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared gravel base
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering visible; surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
